message:
feat(franken_evidence): add galaxy-brain card renderer Levels 0-1 (bd-qaaxt.3)

Level 0: one-liner (max 120 chars), plain or ANSI colored
Level 1: multi-line paragraph with ANSI colors showing expected loss,
  calibration, posterior distribution, top features, sorted losses,
  and fallback warning
Level 1 plain: same content without ANSI escape codes

Deterministic output, stateless renderer.
11 new tests + 1 doc test, all quality gates pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1905,7 +1905,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.1","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Capture baseline p50/p95/p99 for all primary operations","description":"Run comprehensive benchmarks for all primary Asupersync operations and capture baseline performance metrics. This is the foundation for all future optimization work -- without baselines, we cannot measure improvement or detect regressions.

BACKGROUND (section 0.11 Tail-Latency Decomposition): The Alien CS Graveyard methodology requires statistical benchmarking with p50/p95/p99 percentiles and bootstrap confidence intervals. Tail-latency decomposition identifies which subsystem contributes most to high-percentile latency. For Asupersync, the tail terms that usually dominate are: queueing (scheduler queue depth), synchronization (lock contention), and retries (cancellation retry count).

OPERATIONS TO BENCHMARK: (1) Task spawn -- measure time from spawn call to first poll. (2) Task cancellation -- measure time from cancel signal to all obligations released. (3) Channel send/recv -- measure one-way latency for both bounded and unbounded channels. (4) Cx capability check -- measure has<T>() and get<T>() latency. (5) Budget check -- measure budget remaining check and propagation latency. (6) RaptorQ encode/decode -- measure encoding and decoding throughput at various block sizes. (7) DPOR exploration -- measure exploration rate (states/second) for a standard test program.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Use criterion.rs with sample_size(100) and measurement_time(Duration::from_secs(10)) for stable results. (2) Each benchmark produces: p50, p95, p99 latencies and 95% bootstrap confidence intervals. (3) Store results in artifacts/baseline.json with schema: { operation: string, p50_ns: u64, p95_ns: u64, p99_ns: u64, ci95_lower_ns: u64, ci95_upper_ns: u64, timestamp: string, git_sha: string }. (4) First mandatory counters for tail decomposition: scheduler_queue_depth (histogram), cancellation_retry_count (counter), wake_to_run_latency_us (histogram). Expose via metrics crate with prometheus exporter. (5) Use black_box() to prevent compiler optimization of benchmark targets. (6) Benchmark both cold (first run) and warm (steady state) performance.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Baselines captured for all 7 operations listed above. (2) Each baseline has valid confidence intervals (CI width < 20% of median). (3) Results are reproducible: running baseline twice produces overlapping CIs. (4) artifacts/baseline.json is committed to the repository with git SHA tag. (5) CI can compare new results against baseline and flag >5% regression. (6) Tail-latency counters (queue depth, retry count, wake-to-run) are operational and emitting.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each benchmark run logs: operation_name, sample_count, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, ci95_lower_ns, ci95_upper_ns, git_sha, timestamp, machine_info (cpu, os). Tail-latency counters are emitted as prometheus-style metrics: asupersync_scheduler_queue_depth_histogram, asupersync_cancellation_retry_count_total, asupersync_wake_to_run_latency_us_histogram. Summary report generated as Markdown for PR comments.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Verify benchmark harness itself: a known-cost operation (sleep 1ms) should benchmark at ~1ms +/- 10%. (2) Verify baseline stability: run same benchmark 5 times, assert all p50 values are within 10% of each other. (3) Verify regression detection: artificially degrade an operation by 10%, assert CI pipeline flags it.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:32.998835007Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:49:48.244540585Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["baseline","benchmarks","methodology"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:42Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1397,"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Methodology baselines benchmark suite created (benches/methodology_baselines.rs). 46 benchmarks across 5 groups: task spawn (6), task cancellation (5), channel send/recv (6), Cx capability (11), budget check (18). Operations 6-7 (RaptorQ/DPOR) already covered by existing suites. Generated artifacts/baseline.json. All gates pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:49:48Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.2","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Add golden output checksums for core test suite","description":"Implement golden output checksums for Asupersync's core test suite. Golden checksums catch silent semantic changes that pass assertions but produce different outputs -- a critical gap in traditional testing.

BACKGROUND (section 0 Methodology): The Alien CS Graveyard methodology requires golden output checksums as one of the four CI artifact gates (alongside baseline benchmarks, flamegraphs, and proof notes). Golden checksums provide a deterministic fingerprint of system behavior that detects any change in observable output, even if all assertions still pass.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define a GoldenChecksum struct: { test_name: String, output_hash: String (SHA-256 hex), schema_version: u32, generated_at: String, git_sha: String }. (2) Store checksums in artifacts/golden_checksums.json as a sorted array (stable for git diff). (3) Use sha2 crate for SHA-256 hashing. (4) Capture mechanism: each test that opts into golden checksums calls golden::capture(test_name, &output) which hashes the output and records it. (5) Verification: in CI, golden::verify(test_name, &output) compares against stored checksum and fails with a clear diff if mismatched. (6) Update workflow: GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 cargo test regenerates all checksums. Changed checksums require a justification comment in the PR. (7) Integration with evidence ledger: each checksum change is logged as an EvidenceLedger entry with component=golden_checksums, action=checksum_changed|checksum_verified.

COVERAGE: All unit tests for: obligation system (SendPermit lifecycle, Lease lifecycle, Reserve/Commit lifecycle), channel operations (send/recv ordering, backpressure behavior), scheduler (task ordering under various policies), cancellation protocol (cancel propagation, obligation cleanup), budget propagation (tropical semiring arithmetic), and Lab runtime replay (deterministic scheduling output).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Golden checksums exist for all core test categories listed above. (2) CI fails if any test output diverges from golden checksum (exit code 1 with clear error message). (3) GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 regenerates checksums and outputs a diff summary. (4) Checksum stability: running the same test 100 times produces the same checksum (determinism requirement). (5) Schema version in checksum file enables forward-compatible migration when test output format changes.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each golden checksum verification logs: test_name, expected_hash, actual_hash, match (bool), verification_time_us. On mismatch: log expected vs actual hash, output size, and first differing byte position. Summary at CI completion: total_verified, passed, failed, skipped. All logs in structured JSON for CI parsing. EvidenceLedger entry for each checksum change with justification field.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Meta-test: verify the golden checksum framework itself -- capture a checksum, modify the output, verify detection. (2) Stability test: run all golden tests 10 times, verify all produce identical checksums. (3) Update workflow test: verify GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 correctly regenerates and the diff is human-readable.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:40.821017062Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:59:29.335400578Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["golden-checksums","methodology","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:15Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":1398,"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.2","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Golden checksum JSON artifact and GOLDEN_UPDATE workflow landed (ac61bcc). 14 checksums across 6 subsystems in artifacts/golden_checksums.json. 4 new scenarios: budget combine_chain, deadline_check_matrix, obligation send_permit_lifecycle, region_cancel_propagation. GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 workflow for regeneration. All gates pass.","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:59:29Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.3","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Integrate EvidenceLedger schema for runtime decisions","description":"Integrate the canonical EvidenceLedger schema into Asupersync's runtime decision points. Every runtime decision (scheduler policy selection, budget allocation, cancellation strategy, capability check) must emit an EvidenceLedger entry with: ts_unix_ms, component, action, posterior (Bayesian belief state), expected_loss_by_action (decision-theoretic loss for each option), chosen_expected_loss, calibration_score, fallback_active, top_features. Implementation: (1) Define EvidenceLedger trait with emit() method, (2) Implement JSONL backend for offline replay, (3) Add evidence emission to scheduler, cancellation protocol, budget system, and capability system, (4) Add evidence_ledger field to Cx for automatic context propagation. Acceptance criteria: All runtime decisions are logged with sufficient detail for offline replay and decision audit.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:18:50.398870552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:06:26.788605538Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["evidence-ledger","methodology","observability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.3","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1e2if.4","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Add property-based testing (proptest) for all parsers and protocols","description":"Add comprehensive property-based testing using proptest for all parsers and protocol implementations in Asupersync. Coverage targets: (1) Message parsers — roundtrip property (parse(serialize(msg)) == msg), (2) Protocol state machines — all reachable states are valid (no panic on any input sequence), (3) Budget arithmetic — tropical semiring laws (associativity, commutativity, identity), (4) Severity lattice — lattice laws (idempotency, commutativity, associativity of join/meet), (5) Channel operations — linearizability (concurrent operations are equivalent to some sequential history), (6) Cancellation protocol — cancel-correctness (cancelled tasks release all obligations). Each proptest must use shrinking to produce minimal failing cases. Configure: 10,000 cases for fast tests, 1,000,000 for nightly. Store regression cases in proptest-regressions/ directory for deterministic replay.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:00.924753412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:00.924753412Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["methodology","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"}]}

Coverage targets: (1) Message parsers — roundtrip property (parse(serialize(msg)) == msg), (2) Protocol state machines — all reachable states are valid (no panic on any input sequence), (3) Budget arithmetic — tropical semiring laws (associativity, commutativity, identity), (4) Severity lattice — lattice laws (idempotency, commutativity, associativity of join/meet), (5) Channel operations — linearizability (concurrent operations are equivalent to some sequential history), (6) Cancellation protocol — cancel-correctness (cancelled tasks release all obligations). Each proptest must use shrinking to produce minimal failing cases. Configure: 10,000 cases for fast tests, 1,000,000 for nightly. Store regression cases in proptest-regressions/ directory for deterministic replay.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:00.924753412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T19:01:18.263962491Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["methodology","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:44Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.5","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Set up CI artifact gates (baseline, flamegraph, golden checksums, proof note)","description":"Set up CI artifact gates that block merges unless all required artifacts are present and valid. This enforces the Alien CS Graveyard section 0 methodology at the CI level -- no PR can merge without producing the required evidence artifacts.

BACKGROUND (section 0 CI Gates): The methodology requires four mandatory artifacts per PR: (1) Baseline benchmark comparison, (2) Flamegraph for changed hot paths, (3) Golden checksum validation, (4) Proof note for safety-critical changes. These gates prevent regression without manual review overhead.

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) GitHub Actions workflow with four separate jobs, one per gate. (2) Benchmark gate: run criterion.rs benchmarks, use critcmp or criterion-compare to diff against main branch baselines in artifacts/baseline.json. Fail if any p99 metric regresses by >5% with statistical significance (non-overlapping 95% CIs). (3) Flamegraph gate: detect changed files in hot-path modules (src/scheduler/, src/channel/, src/obligation/). If changed, require a flamegraph artifact in artifacts/flamegraphs/<pr_id>.svg. Generate with cargo flamegraph --freq 997 (prime frequency avoids aliasing). (4) Golden checksum gate: run all golden-checksummed tests and verify against artifacts/golden_checksums.json. Any mismatch fails the gate. (5) Proof note gate: if the PR touches files matching src/obligation/*, src/safety/*, or any file with unsafe blocks, require artifacts/proof_notes/<pr_id>.md with a formal argument for correctness. (6) Summary comment: post a PR comment with pass/fail status for each gate, benchmark comparison table, and links to artifacts.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All four gates run on every PR. (2) Gates block merge when requirements are not met (required status checks). (3) Clear, actionable error messages when a gate fails (what failed, how to fix). (4) Flamegraph gate correctly identifies hot-path changes via file path matching. (5) Proof note gate does not trigger for non-safety-critical changes. (6) Summary comment is posted within 2 minutes of CI completion. (7) Gate configuration is version-controlled (not in GitHub UI).

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each gate logs: gate_name, status (pass|fail|skip), duration_ms, artifact_path, failure_reason (if applicable). Summary log: total_gates, passed, failed, skipped, total_ci_time_ms. PR comment includes: benchmark comparison table (operation, main_p99, pr_p99, delta_pct, status), golden checksum summary (total, passed, failed), proof note status (required/provided/not_required). All structured as JSON for CI dashboard ingestion.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Test each gate independently with mock artifacts (pass and fail cases). (2) Integration test: create a test PR branch, run the full CI pipeline, verify all gates execute. (3) Regression test: verify that removing a required artifact causes the correct gate to fail.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:09.725984369Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:32:23.167894529Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","gates","methodology"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.5","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.6","title":"[METHODOLOGY] Implement Decision Contract for scheduler (state/action/loss/calibration/fallback)","description":"Implement the decision-theoretic scheduler contract from the Alien CS Graveyard methodology (section 0). The scheduler currently uses ad-hoc heuristics for policy selection. Replace this with principled expected-loss minimization using Bayesian decision theory.

BACKGROUND (section 0 Decision Contracts): Every runtime decision point must define: (1) State space S -- the observable states the system can be in, (2) Action set A -- the actions the decision-maker can take, (3) Loss matrix L(s,a) -- the cost of taking action a when the true state is s, (4) Posterior update rule -- how observations update beliefs about the current state, (5) Fallback policy -- what to do when the model is uncalibrated or uncertain.

SCHEDULER CONTRACT SPECIFICATION: State space S = {healthy, congested, unstable, partitioned}. Action set A = {aggressive_schedule, balanced_schedule, conservative_schedule}. Loss matrix: L(healthy, aggressive) = 1 (optimal), L(congested, aggressive) = 22 (overloads), L(unstable, aggressive) = 15, L(partitioned, aggressive) = 30 (catastrophic), L(healthy, conservative) = 11 (wasted capacity), L(congested, conservative) = 3, L(unstable, conservative) = 5, L(partitioned, conservative) = 8. Action selection: choose argmin_a E[L(S,a)] = argmin_a sum_s posterior(s) * L(s,a). Posterior updated from rolling conformal coverage on SLA misses. Fallback trigger: coverage drift >3% for 3 consecutive windows or e-process breach (sequential test for distribution shift).

RUST IMPLEMENTATION GUIDANCE: (1) Define SchedulerState enum with variants Healthy, Congested, Unstable, Partitioned. (2) Define SchedulerAction enum with variants AggressiveSchedule, BalancedSchedule, ConservativeSchedule. (3) Implement DecisionContract trait: fn state_space() -> Vec<S>, fn action_set() -> Vec<A>, fn loss(state: S, action: A) -> f64, fn update_posterior(obs: Observation) -> Posterior, fn choose_action(posterior: Posterior) -> (A, f64). (4) Use nalgebra for posterior vector operations. (5) Conformal coverage: maintain a rolling window of (predicted_quantile, actual_outcome) pairs; compute coverage = fraction of outcomes within predicted interval. (6) E-process: implement a running product martingale that crosses threshold 1/alpha when null hypothesis (no drift) is rejected. (7) Fallback: when triggered, switch to conservative_schedule and emit WARN-level alert.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) Scheduler selects action with minimum expected loss given posterior. (2) Posterior updates correctly from observations (verified against hand-computed examples). (3) Conformal coverage drift detection triggers fallback within 3 windows of actual drift. (4) E-process has correct Type I error rate (alpha=0.05, verified by simulation). (5) Loss matrix values are configurable at runtime via TOML config. (6) Decision latency < 10 microseconds (no allocation in hot path). (7) All decisions logged to EvidenceLedger with full posterior, loss-by-action, and calibration score.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Every scheduler decision emits an EvidenceLedger entry: ts_unix_ms, component=scheduler, action=chosen_action, posterior=[p_healthy, p_congested, p_unstable, p_partitioned], expected_loss_by_action={aggressive: X, balanced: Y, conservative: Z}, chosen_expected_loss, calibration_score (conformal coverage), fallback_active. Galaxy-brain card Level 0: scheduler chose balanced_schedule (EL=3.2, cal=0.91). Trace span: scheduler::decide with otel attributes for distributed tracing.

TESTING APPROACH: (1) Unit tests: verify argmin selection with known posteriors and loss matrix. (2) Property-based tests: for any posterior, the chosen action has minimal expected loss (proptest). (3) Simulation test: generate 10000 state transitions, verify posterior tracks true state distribution. (4) Calibration test: verify conformal coverage maintains target rate under stationary conditions. (5) Fallback test: inject distribution shift, verify fallback triggers within 3 windows. (6) Benchmark: verify decision latency < 10us with criterion.rs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:19:17.141504255Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:59:26.820315826Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["decision-contract","methodology","scheduler"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.6","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3ai21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:37Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1e2if.7","title":"Unit and integration tests for Methodology framework adoption","description":"Comprehensive test suite verifying that the section 0 Alien-Artifact Implementation Framework is correctly adopted across Asupersync. This tests the methodology infrastructure itself -- the baselines, golden checksums, evidence ledger integration, and CI gates.

BACKGROUND: The methodology framework (bd-1e2if) establishes four pillars: performance baselines, golden output checksums, evidence ledger integration, and CI artifact gates. Each pillar needs its own validation tests to ensure the methodology enforcement is working correctly.

UNIT TESTS:
(1) Baseline infrastructure: verify that criterion.rs benchmarks produce valid JSON output matching the baseline schema ({ operation, p50_ns, p95_ns, p99_ns, ci95_lower_ns, ci95_upper_ns, timestamp, git_sha }). Test with a known-cost operation and verify latency is within expected bounds.
(2) Golden checksum framework: verify the capture/verify workflow. Capture a checksum, verify it passes. Modify the output, verify the check fails with a clear error message. Test GOLDEN_UPDATE=1 regeneration.
(3) EvidenceLedger integration: verify all decision points that should emit EvidenceLedger entries actually do. Mock the ledger sink, trigger each decision type (scheduler, cancellation, budget, capability), verify entries appear with all required fields.
(4) Property-based test infrastructure: verify proptest configuration (case count, shrinking, regression directory) matches the standard defined in bd-1e2if.4.
(5) Decision Contract validation: verify the DecisionContract trait implementations (bd-1e2if.6) satisfy required invariants: loss matrix is non-negative, posterior sums to 1.0, argmin selection is correct.
(6) Opportunity Matrix Gate: verify that only implementations with opportunity score >= 2.0 pass the gate. Test with scores above and below threshold.

INTEGRATION TESTS:
(7) Full methodology pipeline: make a code change to a hot-path module, run the CI pipeline locally (simulated), verify all four gates fire and produce correct artifacts.
(8) Regression detection: artificially degrade a benchmark by 10%, run baseline comparison, verify the gate catches it.
(9) Evidence ledger end-to-end: run a realistic workload, collect all EvidenceLedger entries, verify they form a complete audit trail of all runtime decisions.
(10) Cross-subsystem consistency: verify that baselines, golden checksums, and evidence ledger entries are all tagged with the same git SHA and timestamp.

LOGGING REQUIREMENTS: Each test logs: test_name, methodology_pillar (baseline|golden|evidence|ci_gate), assertion_count, pass_fail, execution_time_ms. On failure: log the specific methodology requirement that was violated. CI summary: pillars_tested, tests_passed, tests_failed, coverage_by_pillar.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA: (1) All four methodology pillars have dedicated validation tests. (2) Tests detect intentional methodology violations (missing baseline, wrong checksum, missing evidence entry, bypassed gate). (3) Tests complete in under 3 minutes on CI. (4) At least one test per methodology pillar is a property-based test.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:34:54.196004371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:06:08.018772866Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["framework","methodology","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1e2if.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1e2if.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3ai21","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:11Z","created_by":"import"}]}
@@ -2024,7 +2024,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1tiq","title":"[AA][Sched] Work‑stealing with deterministic fallback","description":"## Goal
Design a multi‑queue or work‑stealing scheduler that preserves determinism when required.

## Why
We want throughput without sacrificing replayability. Deterministic mode must be preserved.

## Deliverables
- Work‑stealing design with deterministic fallback.
- Contention analysis + fairness metrics.
- Benchmarks for throughput scaling.

## Acceptance
- Throughput improves under multi‑core workloads.
- Deterministic mode remains bit‑for‑bit reproducible.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CloudyReef","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:08:16.838624341Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T12:15:56.486469385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T12:15:56.486400457Z","close_reason":"Work-stealing with deterministic fallback fully implemented: ThreeLaneWorker has batch work-stealing via DetRng, SchedulerMode::Deterministic + ScheduleCertificate for reproducibility, cancel-lane preemption with bounded fairness (PreemptionMetrics), 140 scheduler tests pass, benchmarks confirm throughput scaling.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-1iva","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-37l1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:22Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tiq","depends_on_id":"bd-3nod","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:22Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1ufh","title":"Review bd-1l7k perf changes for correctness/regression","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BoldDune","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:46:12.562051235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T15:33:22.525112801Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T15:33:22.525037101Z","close_reason":"Review complete: no regressions found","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["performance","raptorq","review"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ufh","depends_on_id":"bd-1l7k","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:48Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1usdh","title":"[FOUNDATION] franken_kernel crate — Suite-Wide Type Substrate","description":"Implement the franken_kernel crate that defines canonical types used across ALL FrankenSuite projects: TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, and the Cx type. No other crate may define competing versions. This is the suite workspace architecture's foundation — dependency firewall rule: /dp/* crates depend on franken_kernel, not the reverse. Build-time enforcement: CI fails if any crate defines forked types.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:51.516645669Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T09:21:51.516645669Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","types"]}
-{"id":"bd-1usdh.1","title":"[FOUNDATION] Define canonical TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion types","description":"Define the canonical identifier and version types for the franken_kernel crate. These types are used across ALL FrankenSuite projects for cross-project tracing, decision logging, and schema compatibility. TraceId: 128-bit unique identifier (UUIDv7 format for time-ordered generation), implements Display, FromStr, Serialize, Deserialize, Hash, Eq, Ord. DecisionId: 128-bit identifier linking a runtime decision to its EvidenceLedger entry, same trait implementations. PolicyId: identifies a decision policy (scheduler, cancellation, budget), includes version for policy evolution tracking. SchemaVersion: semantic version (major.minor.patch) with compatibility checking -- SchemaVersion::is_compatible(other) returns true iff major versions match. All types must be: (1) Copy (128-bit fits in registers), (2) Send + Sync (safe for cross-thread use), (3) no_std compatible (usable in embedded contexts), (4) Zero-cost abstraction over [u8; 16]. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: franken_kernel::type_init (one-time at crate init). DEBUG: type constructed with value -- emitted only when debug_assertions enabled. WARN: SchemaVersion::is_compatible() returned false -- signals schema migration needed. INFO: TraceId::new() at trace propagation boundaries only. Metrics: trace_id_generation_rate (counter), schema_version_compat_check_total (counter), schema_version_compat_failure_total (counter). All metrics exposed via metrics crate with prometheus-compatible naming.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:00.814450673Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:56:06.739679698Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","types"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:36Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1usdh.2","title":"[FOUNDATION] Define canonical Cx type for suite-wide use","description":"Define the canonical Cx (capability context) type in franken_kernel for suite-wide use. Cx is the core abstraction that threads through all Asupersync operations -- it carries capabilities, budget, deadline, trace context, and evidence ledger reference. The franken_kernel Cx must be: (1) Generic over capability set -- Cx<C: CapabilitySet> so each project defines its own capabilities, (2) Lifetime-bound -- Cx<'a, C> ensures capabilities cannot outlive their scope, (3) Propagation-aware -- child Cx inherits parent capabilities (attenuated via Macaroons), (4) Budget-aware -- carries remaining budget in tropical semiring (min, +), (5) Trace-aware -- carries TraceId for cross-project distributed tracing. Must define the CapabilitySet trait with methods: has<T>(), get<T>(), attenuate<T>(caveat). This is the most critical type in the entire FrankenSuite. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: cx::new with parent_trace_id, budget_remaining_ms, capability_count. Span: cx::propagate when creating child Cx from parent. DEBUG: Cx<C> created with trace_id={id}, budget={budget_ms}ms, capabilities=[{cap_names}]. DEBUG: Cx attenuated: capability={cap_name}, caveat={caveat_desc}. INFO: Cx budget exhausted: trace_id={id}, original_budget={orig}ms, elapsed={elapsed}ms -- emitted once per exhaustion event. WARN: Cx capability check failed: trace_id={id}, required={cap_name}, available=[{available_caps}]. Metrics: cx_creation_total (counter), cx_propagation_total (counter), cx_budget_exhaustion_total (counter), cx_capability_check_total (counter by result=granted|denied), cx_budget_remaining_ms (histogram at propagation boundaries).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:12.440993822Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:00:47.828752761Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cx","foundation","franken-kernel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.2","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:39Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1usdh.3","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement build-time enforcement for type uniqueness","description":"Implement build-time enforcement that prevents any FrankenSuite crate from defining types that compete with franken_kernel canonical types. This prevents the forked types anti-pattern where multiple crates define their own TraceId, leading to conversion overhead and confusion. Implementation approaches: (1) CI lint: A custom clippy-like lint (or script) that scans all crate sources for struct definitions named TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, or Cx, and fails if found outside franken_kernel, (2) Workspace-level cargo deny: Use cargo-deny to enforce that only franken_kernel exports these type names, (3) Documentation: Add clear comments in franken_kernel explaining that these types are canonical and MUST NOT be forked. Acceptance criteria: A PR that adds struct TraceId to any crate other than franken_kernel is rejected by CI with a clear error message. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: INFO: type uniqueness check passed -- scanned {crate_count} crates, found 0 forks. ERROR: type fork detected in crate {crate_name}: found competing definition of {type_name} at {file}:{line}. WARN: type re-export detected in crate {crate_name} -- re-exports are allowed but should use pub use franken_kernel::{type_name}. Metrics: type_fork_scan_duration_ms (histogram), type_fork_count (gauge, target: 0), crates_scanned_total (counter). CI integration: scan runs as a pre-merge check, logs results as structured JSON to CI artifact directory.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:23.529057799Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:00.836990578Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","enforcement","foundation","franken-kernel"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.3","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:10Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1usdh.4","title":"[FOUNDATION] Unit and integration tests for franken_kernel crate","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the franken_kernel crate (TraceId, DecisionId, PolicyId, SchemaVersion, Cx type).

**Unit tests:**
(1) TraceId UUIDv7 ordering — generate 10,000 TraceIds in sequence; verify they are monotonically ordered (UUIDv7 encodes timestamp in the high bits).
(2) TraceId roundtrip — Display -> FromStr roundtrip for random TraceIds; verify equality. Also test Serialize -> Deserialize roundtrip.
(3) DecisionId/PolicyId parity — same roundtrip and ordering tests as TraceId (shared underlying [u8; 16] representation).
(4) SchemaVersion compatibility — verify is_compatible() returns true for same major version, false for different major version. Test edge cases: 0.x vs 0.y, pre-release versions.
(5) SchemaVersion ordering — verify Ord implementation matches semver ordering (1.0.0 < 1.0.1 < 1.1.0 < 2.0.0).
(6) Copy semantics — verify all types are Copy by passing by value twice without clone (compile-time check via trybuild).
(7) Send + Sync — verify all types are Send + Sync (compile-time check).
(8) no_std compatibility — verify crate compiles with no_std (conditional compilation test).
(9) Cx generic capability set — verify Cx<C> works with custom CapabilitySet implementations; test has<T>(), get<T>(), attenuate().
(10) Cx lifetime safety — verify that Cx<'a, C> cannot outlive its scope (compile-fail test via trybuild).
(11) Cx budget propagation — verify that child Cx inherits parent budget in tropical semiring (min, +); test budget exhaustion.
(12) Cx trace propagation — verify that child Cx inherits parent TraceId for distributed tracing.

**Integration tests:**
(13) Cross-crate type uniqueness — create a mock workspace with a crate that imports franken_kernel types; verify the types are usable and no forking occurs.
(14) Cx capability attenuation chain — create a capability, attenuate it 10 times with increasingly restrictive caveats; verify that authority monotonically decreases.
(15) Cx with EvidenceLedger — verify that Cx carries an evidence_ledger reference and entries can be emitted through it.

**Property-based tests (proptest):**
(16) All identifier types: Hash consistency — if a == b then hash(a) == hash(b) for random pairs.
(17) SchemaVersion: transitivity of is_compatible().
(18) Cx budget: tropical semiring laws (associativity of min, commutativity of min, identity element).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test logs: test_name, type_under_test, assertion_count, execution_time_us.
- On failure, log the specific input that caused the assertion failure with full Debug output.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- All roundtrip properties hold for 10,000 random inputs.
- All compile-fail tests produce clear, understandable error messages.
- Tropical semiring laws hold for 100,000 random budget values.
- Cx lifetime safety is enforced at compile time (no runtime checks needed).

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for all random generation with custom strategies for each type.
- Use trybuild for compile-fail and compile-pass tests.
- Use tempfile for no_std compilation tests (cargo build with custom target).
- Tests must be runnable with both std and no_std configurations.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:11.959293410Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T17:22:42.941834446Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-kernel","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1usdh.4","depends_on_id":"bd-1usdh.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:43Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.1","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement EvidenceLedger struct with serde serialization","description":"Implement the core EvidenceLedger struct in the franken_evidence crate with full serde serialization support. Fields: ts_unix_ms: u64, component: String, action: String, posterior: Vec<f64>, expected_loss_by_action: HashMap<String, f64>, chosen_expected_loss: f64, calibration_score: f64, fallback_active: bool, top_features: Vec<(String, f64)>. Must implement: Serialize, Deserialize, Clone, Debug. Serialization format must be compact (use short field names in serde rename) for JSONL efficiency. Include a builder pattern for ergonomic construction. Include validation: posterior must sum to ~1.0, calibration_score in [0,1], expected losses non-negative. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: evidence_ledger::emit with component, action, trace_id. DEBUG: EvidenceLedger entry constructed: component={component}, action={action}, posterior_len={n}, calibration={cal:.3f}. WARN: EvidenceLedger validation failed: {field_name} out of range ({value} not in [{min}, {max}]). ERROR: EvidenceLedger builder missing required field: {field_name}. INFO: EvidenceLedger entry emitted (logged at the emission sink, not at struct construction). Metrics: evidence_ledger_entries_total (counter by component), evidence_ledger_validation_failures_total (counter by field), evidence_ledger_entry_size_bytes (histogram), evidence_ledger_calibration_score (gauge by component).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:39.368991303Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:54:23.851694415Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T18:54:23.851559804Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-evidence","serialization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:14Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.2","title":"[FOUNDATION] Implement JSONL exporter for offline replay","description":"Implement the JSONL exporter for the EvidenceLedger in franken_evidence. The exporter writes one JSON object per line to an append-only file, enabling efficient offline replay and analysis of runtime decisions. Implementation: (1) JsonlExporter struct with buffered file writer, (2) Append-only semantics -- never modify existing lines, (3) Atomic flush -- use write + fsync to prevent partial lines on crash, (4) Rotation -- support file rotation by size (default 100MB) or time (default 1 hour), (5) Compression -- optional zstd streaming compression for rotated files, (6) Schema version header -- first line of each file is a schema version record. Performance targets: <1us per record append, <10MB/hour at 1000 decisions/second. Must handle concurrent writers, disk full, and crash recovery. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Span: jsonl_exporter::write with file_path, entry_count. Span: jsonl_exporter::rotate with reason (size|time), old_file, new_file. DEBUG: JSONL entry written: offset={byte_offset}, size={entry_bytes}, flush_latency_us={lat}. INFO: JSONL file rotated: reason={reason}, old_file={path}, old_size={bytes}, entries_written={n}. WARN: JSONL write latency exceeded threshold: latency_us={lat}, threshold_us={thresh} -- indicates I/O contention. WARN: disk space low: available_bytes={avail}, threshold_bytes={thresh}. ERROR: JSONL write failed: error={err}, dropping entry (no blocking). ERROR: crash recovery: partial last line detected at offset={offset}, skipping {bytes} bytes. Metrics: jsonl_write_latency_us (histogram), jsonl_entries_written_total (counter), jsonl_bytes_written_total (counter), jsonl_rotation_total (counter by reason), jsonl_write_errors_total (counter), jsonl_crash_recovery_skipped_bytes (counter).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:22:50.548741988Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:01:27.865938499Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","foundation","franken-evidence","jsonl"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:17Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.4","title":"[FOUNDATION] Unit and integration tests for franken_evidence crate","description":"Comprehensive test suite for the franken_evidence crate (EvidenceLedger struct, JSONL exporter, galaxy-brain renderer).

**Unit tests:**
(1) EvidenceLedger struct validation — posterior must sum to ~1.0 (within 1e-6 epsilon), calibration_score in [0,1], expected losses non-negative. Use proptest to generate random entries and verify validation catches all invalid states.
(2) Serde roundtrip — serialize to JSON and deserialize back; verify equality for all field types including Vec<f64> posteriors and HashMap<String, f64> loss maps.
(3) Builder pattern ergonomics — verify that the builder produces valid entries and that required fields cause compile errors when omitted (trybuild).
(4) JSONL exporter append-only semantics — write N entries, verify file has exactly N lines, each parseable as a valid EvidenceLedger entry.
(5) JSONL exporter crash recovery — write N entries, truncate the file mid-line, verify the reader skips the partial last line and recovers all N-1 complete entries.
(6) JSONL rotation — write entries until rotation threshold (100MB or 1 hour), verify new file created with schema version header, old file correctly closed.
(7) Zstd compression — verify that compressed rotated files decompress to identical content as uncompressed originals.
(8) Concurrent writer safety — spawn 10 threads writing simultaneously; verify no interleaved lines, no data corruption, all entries recoverable.
(9) Galaxy-brain Level 0 — verify one-liner format contains action, expected loss, and calibration score.
(10) Galaxy-brain Level 1 — verify paragraph format contains all summary fields in human-readable layout.
(11) Galaxy-brain Level 2 — verify ASCII histogram is generated for posterior distribution with correct bin widths.
(12) Galaxy-brain Level 3 — verify diff output when given two consecutive entries from the same component.

**Integration tests:**
(13) Full pipeline — create entries via builder, write to JSONL, read back, render at all 4 galaxy-brain levels; verify end-to-end integrity.
(14) Schema version migration — create a JSONL file with v1 schema, read with v2 schema reader; verify graceful handling (migration or clear error).

**Logging requirements:**
- Each test logs: test_name, entry_count, file_size_bytes, write_latency_p99_us, read_latency_p99_us.
- On corruption detection, log byte offset and hex dump of corrupted region.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Serde roundtrip holds for 10,000 random entries.
- Crash recovery loses at most 1 entry (the partial last line).
- Concurrent writes produce zero corruption across 100 runs with 10 threads each.
- Galaxy-brain output is deterministic (golden checksum stable).

**Rust implementation notes:**
- Use proptest for random entry generation with custom strategies.
- Use tempfile for isolated file I/O tests.
- Use loom for concurrent writer testing (if lock-free internals).
- Use insta for snapshot testing of galaxy-brain rendered output.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T17:21:49.949249324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:29.064245197Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["foundation","franken-evidence","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-qaaxt.4","depends_on_id":"bd-qaaxt.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:30:20Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-qaaxt.5","title":"[FOUNDATION] Galaxy-brain Level 2-3 advanced renderers (histogram, diff, HTML)","description":"Implement the advanced galaxy-brain card renderers (Levels 2 and 3) as a follow-on to the core Level 0-1 renderers. This bead is split from bd-qaaxt.3 to keep each bead to 1-3 days of work. SCOPE: Level 2 (full card): ASCII histogram for posterior distribution with correct bin widths, loss matrix table, feature importance bar chart, and calibration visualization. Level 3 (debug): Full JSON dump with diff from previous decision for same component -- requires a sliding window of recent entries. HTML renderer: generates HTML cards for dashboard embedding with inline CSS. Markdown renderer: generates GitHub-flavored markdown for PR comments. TRACING / LOGGING SPECIFICATION: Same contract as parent bd-qaaxt.3 -- Span: galaxy_brain::render with level, component. DEBUG: histogram bin computation for posterior with {n} elements, bin_width={w}. DEBUG: Level 3 diff: prev_entry_age_ms={age}, changed_fields={n}. INFO: advanced renderer initialized with sliding_window_size={n}. WARN: Level 3 diff unavailable: no previous entry for component={component}.
